page: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense storage instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor running on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 61.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core. A key hardware characteristic is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 12,288 GB of local HDD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and virtualization is managed via the Xen hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the d2.2xlarge is optimized for high-capacity storage density rather than raw computational speed. Single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the processor performance in the lower tiers, categorized as poor to weak. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block read operations but weak results for write operations. Database and key-value store performance, including Redis SET operations, also fall into the lower percentiles, reflecting the legacy architecture of the Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d2.2xlarge offers a high volume of local HDD storage relative to its compute and memory resources, making it a specialized option for workloads requiring massive sequential disk access without demanding high CPU performance. The instance is best suited for storage-intensive applications, such as data warehousing, MapReduce jobs, Hadoop clusters, log processing, and large-scale file systems where storage capacity per resource is prioritized over high IOPS or compute speed.
description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for workloads requiring high local storage capacity. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 61.0 GB of RAM. The defining feature of this instance is its 12,288 GB of local HDD storage, supported by a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates modest CPU and memory performance, with single-core and multi-core metrics falling into the lower percentiles. Consequently, the d2.2xlarge is highly cost-efficient for storage-dense applications but less suited for compute-heavy tasks. It is ideal for data warehousing, MapReduce, Hadoop clusters, and large-scale log processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and 12,288 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for data warehousing and large-scale file systems.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d2.2xlarge is a dense storage instance with 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB RAM, and 12,288 GB local HDD storage for data-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for large-scale data processing and warehousing.
bullet_points: Features 12,288 GB of local HDD storage for high-capacity data requirements.
Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor with 8 vCPUs and 61.0 GB RAM.
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on a Xen hypervisor.
Optimized for sequential read and write storage workloads rather than high-compute performance.
Best suited for Hadoop, MapReduce, data warehousing, and log processing.
